ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for chocolate marble milk toast; mapped to the closest parent class 'bread food product' (FOODON_00002683) as the most defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for chocolate marble milk toast; A000E (Bread and similar bakery products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for chocolate marble milk toast; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of chocolate marble milk toast, approximately 60 g, which is roughly one-eighth of a standard 480 g loaf.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate Marble Milk Toast — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient data (G00010099); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18069 (Bread, white, commercially prepared) for saturated fat and micronutrient cross-check
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are taken directly from the HPB Foodo record G00010099. The HPB record reports anomalously low energy (42 kcal/100g) and protein (0 g/100g) relative to typical enriched milk bread (280–340 kcal/100g, 7–9 g protein/100g); however, these values are preserved as-reported from the canonical HPB source and flagged in notes. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy 18069 (white bread, commercially prepared) as the closest approved analogue, since HPB does not report saturated fat for this item. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients are set to -1 where neither HPB nor USDA SR Legacy provides reliable values for this specific product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on white bread GI (GI=70, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chocolate marble milk toast in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 70 (high) based on white bread (GI=70, glucose scale) as the closest approved analogue, consistent with enriched refined-flour milk bread. The chocolate ganache swirl does not materially lower the GI given the dominant refined-flour matrix. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 7.0 g) / 100 = 4.9. Available carbohydrate per 60 g serving = 10.36 × 0.60 × (1 − 0) ≈ 7.0 g (dietary fibre = 0, so available carb = total carb).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar and refined carbohydrates with negligible dietary fibre and potassium
hypertension.guidance: Although the HPB-reported sodium is very low (3 mg/100g), the high added-sugar content (9.94 g/100g) and near-zero dietary fibre and potassium independently elevate cardiovascular risk; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and prefer wholegrain bread alternatives with higher potassium and fibre content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index refined-flour bread with 9.94 g added sugar per 100 g and zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chocolate marble milk toast is a high-GI (estimated GI≈70) refined-flour enriched bread with significant added sugar and no dietary fibre to slow glucose absorption; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and choose low-GI wholegrain alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and dark chocolate ganache, combined with added sugar and refined carbohydrates
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The butter and dark chocolate ganache in milk toast contribute saturated fat (estimated ~0.08 g/100g per HPB-adapted data, though typical enriched milk bread contains 2–4 g saturated fat/100g); combined with high added sugar and refined flour, regular consumption may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and glycaemic control relevant to cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate Marble Milk Toast (巧克力大理石牛奶吐司) is a soft, enriched white bread loaf popular in Singaporean and Chinese-style bakeries. The dough is made with wheat flour, fresh milk, light cream, condensed milk, fine sugar, butter, and milk powder, producing a pillowy, slightly sweet crumb characteristic of Japanese-influenced Hokkaido or shokupan-style milk bread. A rich, bittersweet dark chocolate ganache is swirled through the dough before baking, creating a distinctive marble pattern and infusing each slice with aromatic cocoa flavour. The loaf is typically baked in a Pullman tin or open-top pan and sliced into thick portions. Nutritionally, the HPB Foodo database records this item at 42 kcal/100g with 10.36 g carbohydrate, 9.94 g sugar, 0.25 g fat, and 0 g protein per 100g — values that are anomalously low compared to typical enriched milk bread profiles (280–340 kcal/100g, 7–9 g protein, 5–8 g fat, 45–55 g carbohydrate). The data are preserved as reported from the canonical HPB source but flagged as potentially erroneous. The bread has a high glycaemic index (estimated GI≈70) due to its refined-flour base and added sugar, with zero dietary fibre, making it unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or seeking low-GI foods. The dark chocolate component provides minimal polyphenol benefit at typical swirl quantities. Best consumed fresh on the day of purchase.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values are sourced directly from the canonical HPB Foodo record G00010099 (confidence 0.95 per input metadata); however, the reported macronutrient profile (42 kcal/100g, 0 g protein, 0.25 g fat) is anomalously low relative to all known enriched milk bread references and is likely a data entry error in the source. Confidence is therefore downgraded to medium. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy 18069 as the closest approved analogue. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: DATA QUALITY FLAG: The HPB Foodo record G00010099 reports energy at 42 kcal/100g, protein at 0 g/100g, fat at 0.25 g/100g, and carbohydrate at 10.36 g/100g. These values are inconsistent with the known composition of enriched milk bread (typical: 280–340 kcal/100g, 7–9 g protein, 5–8 g fat, 45–55 g carbohydrate). The data are preserved as reported from the canonical HPB source per Project Veritas source-fidelity policy, but a data correction review is strongly recommended before clinical or consumer use. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is reported as 0.0 per the HPB canonical source (G00010099); confirmed zero per HPB, though flagged as a probable data error — typical enriched milk bread contains 7–9 g protein/100g. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB; refined white bread with no added fibre sources is consistent with near-zero fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB; this is plausible given the low fat content as reported, though actual cholesterol from butter and eggs in enriched bread would typically be 20–40 mg/100g. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB (0.0 mg/100g as reported). NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel for this specific chocolate marble milk toast product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16. SATURATED FAT: estimated at 0.08 g/100g by proportional scaling from USDA SR Legacy 18069 (white bread, commercially prepared: 0.589 g saturated fat per 100g) adjusted to the HPB-reported fat content of 0.25 g/100g. GI NOTE: GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread (GI=70, glucose reference) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). No direct GI measurement for chocolate marble milk toast was found in any approved GI source. ONTOLOGY NOTE: foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type are set to 'closest_parent' as no exact term exists for chocolate marble milk toast in either FoodOn or FoodEx2.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that protein_g=0 required zero-macro justification in notes. Ontology match types were incorrect.

Iteration 2: Added comprehensive zero-macro justification for protein_g, dietary_fibre_g, and cholesterol_mg. Added -1 sentinel documentation. Verified aliases consistency. Set ontology match types to 'broader' — rejected by validator as invalid enum value.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent', which is a valid enum value per the scoring model. All other sections preserved from iteration 2. All hard gates now pass.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
